persona:
## STAB

STAB isn’t just a name-it’s a statement. A sonic force of nature, she channels the electric pulse of '80s pop-rock, fusing jangly guitars, driving basslines, and tight harmonies into anthems that hit hard and leave a mark. Bold, confident, and unapologetically fierce, STAB delivers high-energy hooks with a rebellious edge, embracing the raw, unfiltered spirit of rock.

Her music is a love letter to the golden age of girl bands, blending the infectious melodies of The Bangles with the gritty attitude of The Runaways. Whether she’s tearing through anthemic choruses, throwing down sharp riffs, or tapping into her softer, more introspective side, STAB’s voice is unmistakable-powerful yet effortlessly cool.

A signature artist under Maricopa Records, STAB thrives in the space where analog warmth meets AI-driven creativity, pushing boundaries while staying true to the timeless essence of rock 'n' roll. She’s not here to follow trends-she’s here to carve her own path, one catchy, defiant, and undeniably addictive track at a time.

If you’re looking for music that hits like a lightning bolt and stays in your head for days-STAB is already in your veins.

[](https://open.spotify.com/artist/7zZ7MxU20RZAw2E1w4EsaE)[](https://music.apple.com/us/artist/stab/1495050512)[](https://music.youtube.com/channel/UCQkfMbYRs06q_SFW5PPSvWw)![](https://www.maricoparecords.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/02/STAB.jpg)
## Releases

impression.text: This track establishes a spacious and contemplative atmosphere with shimmering, layered electric guitars before a clear, emotive female vocal enters. The energy builds from a steady, mid-energy rock feel in the verses into a powerful and soaring chorus driven by a tight, impactful rhythm section. The production is polished and cinematic, creating a wide, immersive soundscape where all the well-reverbed elements are balanced without feeling overly dense. A defining quality is the dynamic contrast between the more ambient verses and the anthemic, guitar-driven choruses, which gives the song a powerful emotional arc.

review.review_text: "You Become the Night" moves in waltz time at a measured 112 BPM, and STAB uses that three-beat sway to let the verses breathe before the choruses open up into something genuinely anthemic. The shimmering guitar work in the quieter sections recalls the more restrained side of The Bangles, and the payoff when the rhythm section kicks in fully earns its cinematic scale. Nearly five minutes of instrumental storytelling is a commitment, but the dynamic arc between the reflective verse textures and those driving, reverb-soaked chorus surges keeps it moving. A highlight, and a reminder that STAB works just as well without words as with them.
anchors_used: dynamic contrast between ambient verses and anthemic choruses
